1	import os
2	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
3	from collections.abc import Mapping
4	from datetime import datetime
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Literal, cast, override
6	
7	import boto3
8	
9	if TYPE_CHECKING:
10	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient
11	    from types_boto3_dynamodb.type_defs import (
12	        AttributeValueTypeDef,
13	        UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef,
14	    )
15	else:
16	    DynamoDBClient = Any
17	    AttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
18	    UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef = dict[str, Any]
19	
20	
21	from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.erasure_request import ErasureRequest, ErasureState
22	
23	
24	class RequestRepository(ABC):
25	    """Common interface for dealing with Erasure Request storage"""
26	
27	    @abstractmethod
28	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
29	        """Create a new erasure request in the underlying storage"""
30	        raise NotImplementedError()
31	
32	    @abstractmethod
33	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
34	        """
35	        Updates an existing item in the underlying storage based on the given request.
36	
37	        Note that the requests id is used to do the updating.
38	        """
39	        raise NotImplementedError()
40	
41	    @abstractmethod
42	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
43	        """Get a specific request from the underlying storage based upon its id."""
44	        raise NotImplementedError()
45	
46	    @abstractmethod
47	    def get_all_in_state(
48	        self,
49	        state: ErasureState,
50	        limit: int = 100,
51	        from_date: datetime | None = None,
52	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
53	        """
54	        Get the erasure requests within a given state up to a certain limit.
55	
56	        When the limit is reached, that many items are returned. There could be
57	        more items with the given state.
58	
59	        You can optionally set a from_date to filter only results from that
60	        given time and date until now.
61	
62	        Warning: This can return a lot of results if you use a terminal state.
63	
64	        Warning: Implementations may cap the amount of items returned under the
65	        given limit if certain conditions are met. For example, DynamoDB will
66	        stop returning items for a single query if the total size reaches 1 MB.
67	        It is up to implementations as to how much they respect this limit.
68	        For example, a DynamoDB implementation could paginate.
69	
70	        """
71	        raise NotImplementedError()
72	
73	
74	class InMemoryRequestRepository(RequestRepository):
75	    """
76	    An example implementation of a request repository.
77	
78	    This implementation simply stores objects in an internal dictionary.
79	    It does not clone objects.
80	
81	    This implementation is not thread safe.
82	    """
83	
84	    def __init__(self) -> None:
85	        super().__init__()
86	        self._db: dict[str, ErasureRequest] = {}
87	
88	    @override
89	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
90	        if request.id in self._db:
91	            raise ValueError(f"{request.id} already exists")
92	        self._db[request.id] = request
93	
94	    @override
95	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
96	        if request.id not in self._db:
97	            raise ValueError(f"{request.id} does not already exists")
98	        self._db[request.id] = request
99	
100	    @override
101	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
102	        return self._db.get(id)
103	
104	    @override
105	    def get_all_in_state(
106	        self, state: ErasureState, limit: int = 100, from_date: datetime | None = None
107	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
108	        if limit <= 0:
109	            return []
110	        found: list[ErasureRequest] = []
111	        count: int = 0
112	        for _, item in self._db.items():
113	            if item.state == state:
114	                if from_date is not None and item.date_issued < from_date:
115	                    continue
116	                found.append(item)
117	                count += 1
118	                if count >= limit:
119	                    break
120	        return found
121	
122	
123	class DynamoDBRequestRepository(RequestRepository):
124	    """DynamoDB backed repository implementation"""
125	
126	    ID_FIELD: Literal["id"] = "id"
127	    STATE_FIELD: Literal["state"] = "state"
128	    DATE_ISSUED_FIELD: Literal["date_issued"] = "date_issued"
129	    SOURCE_FIELD: Literal["source"] = "source"
130	    ERASURE_DETAILS_FIELD: Literal["erasure_details"] = "erasure_details"
131	
132	    NOT_EXISTS_CONDITION: str = f"attribute_not_exists({ID_FIELD})"
133	    EXISTS_CONDITION: str = f"attribute_exists({ID_FIELD})"
134	
135	    def __init__(
136	        self, table_name: str, index_name: str, dynamodb: DynamoDBClient
137	    ) -> None:
138	        super().__init__()
139	        self.table_name: str = table_name
140	        self.index_name: str = index_name
141	        self.dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = dynamodb
142	
143	    @override
144	    def create(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
145	        item = self.item_from_request(request)
146	        # This should error if there is an item in the table with the given id
147	        _response = self.dynamodb.put_item(
148	            TableName=self.table_name,
149	            Item=item,
150	            ReturnValues="NONE",
151	            ConditionExpression=self.NOT_EXISTS_CONDITION,
152	        )
153	
154	    @override
155	    def update(self, request: ErasureRequest) -> None:
156	        item = self.item_from_request(request)
157	        # This should error if there is NOT an item in the table with the given
158	        # id
159	        _response = self.dynamodb.put_item(
160	            TableName=self.table_name,
161	            Item=item,
162	            ReturnValues="NONE",
163	            ConditionExpression=self.EXISTS_CONDITION,
164	        )
165	
166	    @override
167	    def get(self, id: str) -> ErasureRequest | None:
168	        response = self.dynamodb.get_item(
169	            TableName=self.table_name, Key={self.ID_FIELD: {"S": id}}
170	        )
171	
172	        if "Item" in response:
173	            item: dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef] = response["Item"]
174	            # Convert the item back to a domain object
175	            return self.response_to_request(item)
176	        else:
177	            return None
178	
179	    @override
180	    def get_all_in_state(
181	        self,
182	        state: ErasureState,
183	        limit: int = 100,
184	        from_date: datetime | None = None,
185	    ) -> list[ErasureRequest]:
186	        if limit <= 0:
187	            return []
188	        key_condition: str = "#S = :state"
189	
190	        #  The attribute values being used in the query
191	        attribute_values: Mapping[str, UniversalAttributeValueTypeDef] = {
192	            ":state": {"S": state}
193	        }
194	        # Replacements for attribute names that are reserved keywords
195	        attribute_names: Mapping[str, str] = {"#S": "state"}
196	
197	        if from_date is not None:
198	            # Add on the sort key condition to the key_condition
199	            key_condition += " AND date_issued >= :date"
200	            # Add the sort key value to attribute_values
201	            timestamp = int(from_date.timestamp() * 1000)
202	            attribute_values[":date"] = {"N": str(timestamp)}
203	
204	        paginator = self.dynamodb.get_paginator("query")
205	        pages = paginator.paginate(
206	            TableName=self.table_name,
207	            IndexName=self.index_name,
208	            Select="ALL_ATTRIBUTES",
209	            ScanIndexForward=True,  # explicitly set direction of scan
210	            KeyConditionExpression=key_condition,
211	            ExpressionAttributeNames=attribute_names,
212	            ExpressionAttributeValues=attribute_values,
213	            PaginationConfig={"PageSize": min([100, limit]), "MaxItems": limit},
214	        )
215	
216	        results: list[ErasureRequest] = []
217	        for page in pages:
218	            # iterate pages and add converted results to list
219	            if "Items" not in page:
220	                continue
221	            for item in page["Items"]:
222	                erasure = self.response_to_request(item)
223	                results.append(erasure)
224	
225	        return results
226	
227	    @classmethod
228	    def response_to_request(
229	        cls, response: dict[str, AttributeValueTypeDef]
230	    ) -> ErasureRequest:
231	        """Convert a response dictionary to an ErasureRequest"""
232	        if cls.ID_FIELD not in response:
233	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.ID_FIELD} field")
234	        if cls.STATE_FIELD not in response:
235	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.STATE_FIELD} field")
236	        if cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD not in response:
237	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD} field")
238	        if cls.SOURCE_FIELD not in response:
239	            raise ValueError(f"Missing {cls.SOURCE_FIELD} field")
240	
241	        id: str = response[cls.ID_FIELD][
242	            "S"
243	        ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
244	        state: str = response[cls.STATE_FIELD][
245	            "S"
246	        ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
247	        source: str = response[cls.SOURCE_FIELD][
248	            "S"
249	        ]  # pyright: ignore[reportTypedDictNotRequiredAccess]
250	
251	        date_issued_field = response[cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD]
252	        if "N" not in date_issued_field:
253	            raise ValueError(f"Wrong type for {cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD}")
254	        date_issued_ms = int(date_issued_field["N"])
255	        date_issued: datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(date_issued_ms / 1000)
256	
257	        erasure_details: dict[str, str] = {}
258	        if cls.ERASURE_DETAILS_FIELD in response:
259	            erasure_details_field = response[cls.ERASURE_DETAILS_FIELD]
260	            if "M" in erasure_details_field:
261	                returned_map = erasure_details_field["M"]
262	                for k, v in returned_map.items():  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
263	                    coerced = cast(dict[str, str], v)
264	                    value: str = coerced["S"]
265	                    erasure_details[k] = value
266	
267	        # TODO: Should we validate the fields afterward?
268	        return ErasureRequest(
269	            id=id,
270	            state=state,  # pyright: ignore[reportArgumentType]
271	            date_issued=date_issued,
272	            source=source,
273	            erasure_details=erasure_details,  # pyright: ignore[reportArgumentType]
274	        )
275	
276	    @classmethod
277	    def item_from_request(cls, request: ErasureRequest):
278	        """Convert a request to a DynamoDB Item"""
279	        # Build the map type
280	        erasure_details: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}
281	        for k, v in request.erasure_details.items():
282	            erasure_details[k] = {"S": v}
283	
284	        item = {
285	            cls.ID_FIELD: {"S": request.id},
286	            cls.STATE_FIELD: {"S": request.state.strip().lower()},
287	            cls.DATE_ISSUED_FIELD: {"N": str(request.date_issued_ms)},
288	            cls.SOURCE_FIELD: {"S": request.source},
289	            cls.ERASURE_DETAILS_FIELD: {"M": erasure_details},
290	        }
291	        return item
292	
293	
294	DYNAMO_DB_TABLE_ENV_VAR: Literal["DYNAMO_DB_TABLE"] = "DYNAMO_DB_TABLE"
295	"""
296	Environment variable used for defining the DynamoDB table used when creating
297	from the environment.
298	
299	When testing this variable should not be favoured, instead you should set the
300	table name explicitly.
301	"""
302	
303	DYNAMO_DB_INDEX_ENV_VAR: Literal["DYNAMO_DB_INDEX"] = "DYNAMO_DB_INDEX"
304	"""
305	Environment variable used for defining the DynamoDB index used when creating
306	from the environment.
307	
308	When testing this variable should not be favoured, instead you should set the
309	index name explicitly.
310	"""
311	
312	
313	def get_dyanmodb_request_repository() -> DynamoDBRequestRepository:
314	    """Return a DynamoDB request repository from using environment variables"""
315	
316	    table_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_TABLE_ENV_VAR)
317	    if table_name is None or table_name == "":
318	        raise ValueError(f"{DYNAMO_DB_TABLE_ENV_VAR} was not set")
319	
320	    index_name: str | None = os.getenv(DYNAMO_DB_INDEX_ENV_VAR)
321	    if index_name is None or index_name == "":
322	        raise ValueError(f"{DYNAMO_DB_INDEX_ENV_VAR} was not set")
323	
324	    dynamodb: DynamoDBClient = boto3.client("dynamodb")
325	    return DynamoDBRequestRepository(
326	        table_name=table_name, index_name=index_name, dynamodb=dynamodb
327	    )
328	
329	
330	REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR: Literal["REPOSITORY_TYPE"] = "REPOSITORY_TYPE"
331	"""The environment variable used to force a specific repository type"""
332	
333	
334	def get_request_repository() -> RequestRepository:
335	    """Get a concrete repository via the environment variables"""
336	    repo_type: str | None = os.getenv(REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR)
337	
338	    match repo_type:
339	        case "" | None:
340	            return _get_default_request_repository()
341	        case s:
342	            match s.strip().lower():
343	                case "dynamodb":
344	                    return get_dyanmodb_request_repository()
345	                case "memory":
346	                    return InMemoryRequestRepository()
347	                case "":
348	                    return _get_default_request_repository()
349	                case _:
350	                    raise ValueError(f"Unknown {REPOSITORY_TYPE_ENV_VAR} entry: {s}")
351	
352	
353	def _get_default_request_repository() -> RequestRepository:
354	    """Return a default request repository, this is currently DynamoDB"""
355	    return get_dyanmodb_request_repository()
356

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    audit.log("req-1", "some message")

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []


# --- DynamoDBAuditLogger ---


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)},
                    "message": {"S": "msg"},
                },
            ]
        }
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"
    expected_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    assert client._paginator is not None  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    last_kwargs = client._paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"
    assert last_kwargs["ExpressionAttributeValues"] == {":id": {"S": "req-1"}}
    assert last_kwargs["ScanIndexForward"] is True


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_paginates():
    ts1 = 1778164245000
    ts2 = 1778164305000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts1)},
                    "message": {"S": "first"},
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts2)},
                    "message": {"S": "second"},
                }
            ]
        },
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["first", "second"]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_returns_empty_when_no_items():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=[{"Items": []}])
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    assert logger.get_logs("req-1") == []


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_handles_page_without_items_key():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=[{}])
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    assert logger.get_logs("req-1") == []


# --- AuditEntry ---


def test_audit_entry_to_json():
    ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="hello")

    result = entry.to_json()

    assert result == {
        "id": "req-1",
        "timestamp": "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00",
        "message": "hello",
    }


# --- S3AuditLogReader ---


def _make_stub_s3(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> Any:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    stub = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        stub.get_object.return_value = {  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            "Body": response_body
        }
    return stub


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
            {
                "id": "req-1",
                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
                "message": "second",
            },
        ]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"
    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket",
        prefix="some/prefix",
        s3_client=s3,  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    _ = reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_strips_trailing_slash_from_prefix():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket",
        prefix="some/prefix/",
        s3_client=s3,  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    _ = reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_empty_prefix_omits_slash():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket", prefix="", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    _ = reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="bucket", Key="x.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_key():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchKey")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    assert reader.get_logs("missing-id") == []


def test_s3_reader_returns_empty_on_no_such_bucket():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchBucket")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    assert reader.get_logs("any-id") == []


def test_s3_reader_propagates_other_client_errors():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    with pytest.raises(ClientError):
        _ = reader.get_logs("req-1")


def test_s3_reader_raises_on_malformed_json():
    bad_bytes = gzip.compress(
        b'{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00"\n{bad line'
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=bad_bytes)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket", prefix="pfx", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    with pytest.raises(json.JSONDecodeError):
        _ = reader.get_logs("x")


# --- FallbackAuditLogReader ---


def test_fallback_reader_returns_primary_when_non_empty():
    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    primary.log("req-1", "primary entry", ts)

    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)

    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].message == "primary entry"
    fallback_s3.get_logs.assert_not_called()  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]


def test_fallback_reader_falls_back_to_s3_when_primary_empty():
    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts = datetime(2026, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    expected = [AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="s3 entry")]

    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
    fallback_s3.get_logs.return_value = expected  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]

    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert results == expected
    fallback_s3.get_logs.assert_called_once_with("req-1")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]


def test_fallback_reader_returns_empty_when_both_empty():
    primary = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    fallback_s3 = MagicMock(spec=S3AuditLogReader)
    fallback_s3.get_logs.return_value = []  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]

    reader = FallbackAuditLogReader(primary=primary, fallback=fallback_s3)
    assert reader.get_logs("req-1") == []


# --- Factory functions ---

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    audit.log("req-1", "some message")

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []

def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(

            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},

    ts1 = 1778164245000

# --- AuditEntry ---


def test_audit_entry_to_json():
    ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    entry = AuditEntry(id="req-1", timestamp=ts, message="hello")

# --- S3AuditLogReader ---


def _make_stub_s3(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> Any:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    stub = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        stub.get_object.return_value = {  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            "Body": response_body
        }
    return stub


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [

                "message": "second",

    reader = S3AuditLogReader(

    )

    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []

        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }

    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

            "TableName": "audit-table",

                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]

        table_name="audit-table",

        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)},
                    "message": {"S": "msg"},
                },
            ]
        }

    assert results[0].id == "req-1"

    return stub

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(

) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"

    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client

    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))

def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),

            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },

        }
    ]

        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)},
                    "message": {"S": "msg"},
                },
            ]
        }

    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"

    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    assert client._paginator is not None  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    last_kwargs = client._paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"

    assert last_kwargs["ScanIndexForward"] is True

def _make_stub_s3(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> Any:  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client."""
    from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

    stub = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        stub.get_object.return_value = {  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
            "Body": response_body
        }
    return stub

    assert results[1].message == "second"

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---

    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)

    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    audit.log("req-1", "some message")

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []


# --- DynamoDBAuditLogger ---


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

    assert item == {
        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=original_tz)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
                "req-123",
                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)},
                    "message": {"S": "msg"},
                },
            ]
        }
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"
    expected_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    assert client._paginator is not None  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    last_kwargs = client._paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"
    assert last_kwargs["ExpressionAttributeValues"] == {":id": {"S": "req-1"}}
    assert last_kwargs["ScanIndexForward"] is True

def test_audit_entry_to_json():

# --- S3AuditLogReader ---


def _make_stub_s3(
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> S3Client:
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client typed as S3Client."""
    stub: MagicMock = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body: MagicMock = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body
        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
    return cast(S3Client, stub)


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
            {
                "id": "req-1",
                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
                "message": "second",
            },
        ]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"
    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket",
        prefix="some/prefix",
        s3_client=s3,  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    _ = reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_strips_trailing_slash_from_prefix():

    )

    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="NoSuchKey")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(

    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))


# --- NoOpAuditLogger ---


def test_no_op_log():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    audit.log("id", "message")


def test_no_op_get_logs_returns_empty():
    audit = NoOpAuditLogger()
    assert audit.get_logs("any-id") == []


# --- InMemoryAuditLogger ---


def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_entries_for_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts1 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts2 = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 11, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    audit.log("req-1", "first", ts1)
    audit.log("req-2", "other", ts1)
    audit.log("req-1", "second", ts2)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_chronological_order():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    ts_late = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    # Log out of order
    audit.log("req-1", "late message", ts_late)
    audit.log("req-1", "early message", ts_early)

    results = audit.get_logs("req-1")

    assert [e.message for e in results] == ["early message", "late message"]


def test_in_memory_get_logs_returns_empty_for_unknown_id():
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()
    audit.log("req-1", "some message")

    assert audit.get_logs("unknown-id") == []

def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_create_item_serializes_fields():
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, 123000, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    item = DynamoDBAuditLogger._create_item(  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
        "req-123", "audit message", timestamp
    )

        "id": {"S": "req-123"},
        "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245123"},
        "message": {"S": "audit message"},
    }


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_calls_put_item_with_serialized_item():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )
    timestamp = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    logger.log("req-123", "audit message", timestamp)

    assert client.put_item_calls == [
        {
            "TableName": "audit-table",
            "Item": {
                "id": {"S": "req-123"},
                "timestamp": {"N": "1778164245000"},
                "message": {"S": "audit message"},
            },
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_log_relabels_non_utc_timestamp_before_writing():
    client = StubDynamoDBClient()
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",

                "audit message",
                timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc),
            ),
            "ReturnValues": "NONE",
        }
    ]


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_queries_by_id():
    ts_ms = 1778164245000
    pages = [
        {
            "Items": [
                {
                    "id": {"S": "req-1"},
                    "timestamp": {"N": str(ts_ms)},
                    "message": {"S": "msg"},
                },
            ]
        }
    ]
    client = StubDynamoDBClient(query_pages=pages)
    logger = DynamoDBAuditLogger(
        table_name="audit-table",
        dynamodb=cast(DynamoDBClient, cast(object, client)),
    )

    results = logger.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0].id == "req-1"
    assert results[0].message == "msg"
    expected_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts_ms / 1000, tz=timezone.utc)
    assert results[0].timestamp == expected_ts

    assert client._paginator is not None  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    last_kwargs = client._paginator._last_kwargs  # pyright: ignore[reportPrivateUsage]
    assert last_kwargs["KeyConditionExpression"] == "#id = :id"
    assert last_kwargs["ExpressionAttributeValues"] == {":id": {"S": "req-1"}}
    assert last_kwargs["ScanIndexForward"] is True


def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_paginates():
    ts1 = 1778164245000
    ts2 = 1778164305000
    pages = [

def test_dynamodb_audit_logger_get_logs_returns_empty_when_no_items():

def test_audit_entry_to_json():
    ts = datetime(2026, 5, 7, 14, 30, 45, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

def _make_stub_s3(
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> S3Client:
    """Return a minimal stub for boto3 S3 client typed as S3Client."""
    stub: MagicMock = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body: MagicMock = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body
        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
    return cast(S3Client, stub)


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
            {
                "id": "req-1",
                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
                "message": "second",
            },
        ]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"
    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
    )

import gzip
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
from unittest.mock import MagicMock

import pytest
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from pytest import MonkeyPatch
from types_boto3_dynamodb.client import DynamoDBClient

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from types_boto3_s3.client import S3Client
else:
    S3Client = Any

from dns_de_rte_orchestrator.audit_logging import (
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_BUCKET,
    AUDIT_LOG_S3_PREFIX,
    DYNAMO_DB_AUDIT_LOGGING_TABLE,
    AuditEntry,
    DynamoDBAuditLogger,
    FallbackAuditLogReader,
    InMemoryAuditLogger,
    NoOpAuditLogger,
    S3AuditLogReader,
    get_audit_log_reader,
    get_audit_logger,
    get_dynamodb_audit_logger,
    get_s3_audit_log_reader,
)

_DdbPage = dict[str, Any]  # pyright: ignore[reportExplicitAny]


class StubDynamoDBPaginator:
    _pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, pages: list[_DdbPage]) -> None:
        self._pages = pages
        self._last_kwargs: _DdbPage = {}

    def paginate(self, **kwargs: object) -> list[_DdbPage]:
        self._last_kwargs = dict(kwargs)
        return self._pages


class StubDynamoDBClient:
    _query_pages: list[_DdbPage]

    def __init__(self, query_pages: list[_DdbPage] | None = None) -> None:
        self.put_item_calls: list[dict[str, object]] = []
        self._query_pages = query_pages or []
        self._paginator: StubDynamoDBPaginator | None = None

    def put_item(self, **kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        self.put_item_calls.append(kwargs)
        return {}

    def get_paginator(self, operation: str) -> StubDynamoDBPaginator:
        assert operation == "query"
        self._paginator = StubDynamoDBPaginator(self._query_pages)
        return self._paginator


def stub_get_dynamodb_audit_logger(
    table_name: str, expected: DynamoDBAuditLogger
) -> DynamoDBAuditLogger:
    assert table_name == "audit-table"
    return expected


def stub_boto3_client(
    service_name: str, client: StubDynamoDBClient
) -> StubDynamoDBClient:
    assert service_name == "dynamodb"
    return client


def _make_gzipped_jsonl(entries: list[dict[str, str]]) -> bytes:
    lines = "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in entries)
    return gzip.compress(lines.encode("utf-8"))

def test_in_memory_log():
    before_tests = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    audit = InMemoryAuditLogger()

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    id = "foobar"
    message = "test message1"
    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, timestamp), message)

    message = "message 2"
    audit.log(id, message)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 2
    found_entry = audit.memory_log[1]
    assert found_entry[0][1] >= before_tests
    assert found_entry[0][0] == id
    assert found_entry[1] == message


def test_in_memory_log_change_timezone():

    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 0

    original_tz = timezone(timedelta(hours=12))
    timestamp = datetime.now(original_tz)
    id = "baz"
    message = "testing"

    audit.log(id, message, timestamp)
    assert len(audit.memory_log) == 1

    assert audit.memory_log[0] != ((id, timestamp), message)
    utc_timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    assert audit.memory_log[0] == ((id, utc_timestamp), message)

    ts_early = datetime(2026, 1, 1, 9, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    pages = [

# --- S3AuditLogReader ---


def _make_stub_s3(
    body: bytes | None = None, error_code: str | None = None
) -> MagicMock:
    """Return a minimal MagicMock-based stub for boto3 S3 client.

    Returned as MagicMock (not Any/S3Client) so call sites retain access to
    MagicMock helpers like ``assert_called_once_with`` while keeping the
    helper free of pyright ignores. Cast to S3Client only when handed to
    production code.
    """
    stub: MagicMock = MagicMock()
    if error_code:
        stub.get_object.side_effect = ClientError(
            {"Error": {"Code": error_code, "Message": ""}}, "GetObject"
        )
    elif body is not None:
        response_body: MagicMock = MagicMock()
        response_body.read.return_value = body
        stub.get_object.return_value = {"Body": response_body}
    return stub


def test_s3_reader_returns_entries_from_gzipped_jsonl():
    ts = "2026-05-07T14:30:45+00:00"
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [
            {"id": "req-1", "timestamp": ts, "message": "first"},
            {
                "id": "req-1",
                "timestamp": "2026-05-07T15:00:00+00:00",
                "message": "second",
            },
        ]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="my-bucket", prefix="audit", s3_client=s3  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    results = reader.get_logs("req-1")

    assert len(results) == 2
    assert results[0].message == "first"
    assert results[1].message == "second"
    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="my-bucket", Key="audit/req-1.jsonl.gz"
    )


def test_s3_reader_uses_prefix_in_key():
    data = _make_gzipped_jsonl(
        [{"id": "x", "timestamp": "2026-01-01T00:00:00+00:00", "message": "m"}]
    )
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(body=data)  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    reader = S3AuditLogReader(
        bucket="bucket",
        prefix="some/prefix",
        s3_client=s3,  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
    )

    _ = reader.get_logs("x")

    s3.get_object.assert_called_once_with(  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]
        Bucket="bucket", Key="some/prefix/x.jsonl.gz"

    )

def test_s3_reader_propagates_other_client_errors():
    s3 = _make_stub_s3(error_code="AccessDenied")  # pyright: ignore[reportAny]

def test_fallback_reader_returns_primary_when_non_empty():
